claim: Sakai 2001 'Rational surfaces associated with affine root systems and geometry of the Painleve equations' is the canonical surface-classification framework cited as Comm. Math. Phys. 220 (2001) no. 1, 165-229, DOI 10.1007/s002200100446; the 1999 preprint version is hosted by the Kyoto University Department of Mathematics preprint server (preprint 1999 No. 10) at https://www.math.kyoto-u.ac.jp/preprint/99/10.ps
script: route_findings.md (Phase B.4) + Phase A bibliographic confirmation via DuckDuckGo search and Math Genealogy Project (advisor Michio Jimbo)

claim: SpringerLink direct PDF URL for DOI 10.1007/s002200100446 returns HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/html (3038-byte login redirect page); the route is paywalled and per Rule 1 not auto-authenticated
script: route_findings.md Phase B.1 (Invoke-WebRequest with -UseBasicParsing -MaximumRedirection 5; Content-Type text/html confirms login HTML)
output_hash: n/a (response is dynamic HTML; not slotted)

claim: Kyoto Math Dept preprint server route succeeds: https://www.math.kyoto-u.ac.jp/preprint/99/10.ps returns HTTP 200, Content-Type application/postscript, raw size 1149707 bytes
PostScript file converted to PDF via ps2pdf (MiKTeX 64-bit) produces a 55-page 526933-byte PDF with clean ASCII text-layer extraction (>=99% fidelity) via pdftotext -layout

PDF deposited as slot 13 at tex/submitted/control center/literature/g3b_2026-05-03/13_sakai_1999_preprint_kyoto99_10.pdf with SHA-256 ec1bbda3b77634e6def2a784d04947a0c9631bfade48c72aa0767b22aaf49ed6 and registered in SHA256SUMS.txt with 17-line provenance comment block

Sakai 2001 (1999 preprint) Table 6 (preprint p.30, pdftotext L1733-L1737) classifies the D_6^{(1)} surface as carrying Aut(D_6^{(1)}) semidirect product W((2A_1)^{(1)}) symmetry and corresponding to the Painleve equation P_III^{D_6^{(1)}} (eqs (16)-(18) on the same page)

Sakai 2001 (1999 preprint) eq (18) on p.30 imposes the linear constraint a_1+a_0 = b_1+b_0 = eta on the D_6^{(1)} parameter 4-tuple (a_1, a_0, b_1, b_0); this cuts the parameter space down to a rank-3 hyperplane in R^4
script: extracts.md D.1.b; eq (18) literally reads ds/dt = 4*eta = 4*(a_1+a_0) = 4*(b_1+b_0)

claim: The CT v1.3 sec 3.5 4-tuple (1/6, 0, 0, -1/2), under naive component-wise identification with Sakai's (a_1, a_0, b_1, b_0), violates the Sakai constraint a_1+a_0 = b_1+b_0: a_1+a_0 = 1/6 vs b_1+b_0 = -1/2 (difference 2/3)

Sakai 2001 (1999 preprint) opening summary p.6 (pdftotext L102-L116) lists W((2A_1)^{(1)}) simple-reflection generators w_1, w_0, w_1', w_0' acting on (a_1, a_0, b_1, b_0); w_1 sends (a_1, a_0, b_1, b_0) to (-a_1, a_0+2a_1, b_1, b_0); w_1' sends to (a_1, a_0, -b_1, b_0+2b_1); w_0 and w_0' are conjugates by the diagram automorphism sigma = (6)(24)(50)

All four W((2A_1)^{(1)}) generators (w_1, w_0, w_1', w_0') and the Aut diagram automorphism sigma preserve the linear constraint a_1+a_0 = b_1+b_0 (algebraic check: w_1 preserves a_1+a_0 = a_1+a_0; w_1' preserves b_1+b_0 = b_1+b_0; sigma swaps the two sums, hence preserves the equality)
script: extracts.md D.1.c; manual algebraic check on the four generator formulas

claim: Full-text grep of the 55-page Sakai 2001 (1999 preprint) for any mention of W(B_2), W(C_2), or an explicit homomorphism / covering / quotient between Sakai's W((2A_1)^{(1)}) framing and Okamoto's W(B_2) framing returns ZERO matches in the main body; the only 'extended affine Weyl group' mentions (L3058, L3292) refer to D_5^{(1)} and A_1^{(1)} (P_V and P_II), not to D_6^{(1)} ~ B_2

Sakai 2001 references Okamoto 1987 ('Studies on the Painleve equations I-IV') as bibliographic ref [20] (pdftotext L14-L15) but does NOT state any Weyl-group isomorphism, covering, or quotient between his W((2A_1)^{(1)}) framing of P_III^{D_6^{(1)}} and Okamoto's W(B_2) framing of P_III'

Phase E verdict: UPGRADE_SAKAI_ACQUIRED_W_HOMOMORPHISM_NOT_IN_SAKAI; Sakai 2001 acquired (slot 13); D_6^{(1)} surface classification + W((2A_1)^{(1)}) generators extracted; CT v1.3 4-tuple (1/6, 0, 0, -1/2) confirmed not a direct Sakai parameter point (replicates OKAMOTO-1987-CONSTRAINT-PIN G18 closure); explicit W(B_2) <-> W((2A_1)^{(1)}) homomorphism NOT stated in Sakai 2001 - M6 Phase B.5 anchor is partial, follow-on (Noumi-Yamada 2004 or Witte-Forrester 2010 lecture notes) recommended
